english: the name given in Pur. to Codonopsis ovata, the thick roots of which plant are cooked like turnips or ground and baked; v. klu-mdúd (klu mdud).
I. _paṅ ('pang) (p'aṅ-ma (phang ma), p'aṅ-bu (phang bu) Cs) spindle; paṅ-ló (pang lo), 1. the whirl of the spindle. 2. šiṅ-rtai paṅ-ló (shing rtai pang lo), waggon wheel, Dzl. II. v. paṅ (pang).
Sch., the belly or body of a stringed instrument.

(Glr. also p'oṅs-pa (phongs pa), prov.) to save, to spare, to use economy: srog (srog) to spare one's life; mi-p'aṅs-te (mi phangs te) or -par (-par) e.g. _búl-ba ('bul ba), to give largely, not sparingly; p'aṅ-sems (phang sems), thriftiness; p'aṅ-sems-čan (phang sems can), thrifty, frugal; *p'aṅ-sem čo-če* (*phang sem co ce*), W., to be thrifty, frugal.
stated to be = rin-méd (rin med), Ts.
Ssk., an unmeaning sound, frequently used in magic spells, on which subject Milaraspa speaks rather obstrusely.

a large bag or sack, rás-p'ad (ras phad), rál-p'ad (ral phad), rtsid-p'ad (rtsid phad), sack of cotton cloth, goat's hair, yak's hair; p'ad-k'á (phad kha), -skéd (-sked), -mt'íl (-mthil), the mouth, middle, and bottom of a sack; p'ad-gáṅ (phad gang), a full sack, a sackful; p'ad-stóṅ (phad stong), an empty sack; p'ad-snam (phad snam), sack-twine, sack-cloth; p'ád-tsa (phad tsa), very coarse sack-cloth.

I. sbst., hanging ornaments, lappets of silk, similar to the decorations of our tent-cloths, awnings etc., ka- (ka-), sgo- (sgo-), gduṅ-p'an (gdung phan), on pillars, doors, beams; p'an-gdugs (phan gdugs), a parasol so decorated, S.g. - II. = p'a (pha) II., gen. in the combination of p'an-čád (phan chad) (Glr. also p'an-čód (phan chod)), also p'án-la (phan la) or p'an (phan), towards, until: dá-či-nas dá-lta p'án-la dar čig soṅ (da ci nas da lta phan la dar cig song), from 'but just' till 'just now' a moment has passed, Thgy.; ná-niṅ-nas da p'an-čád lo gčig soṅ (na ning nas da phan chad lo gcig song), Thgy.; *da p'an* (*da phan*), until now, C.; ... nas diṅ-saṅ p'an (-la) (nas ding sang phan (-la)) Glr. from ... till now; p'yi-ma p'an-čád-du gró-ba yin (phyi ma phan chad du 'gro ba yin), I am proceeding towards the future, Thgy.; p'an-čád (phan chad) also beyond: *de p'en-čé' ma do* (*de phen che' ma do*) C. do not go any farther than that place; combined with its contrary tsun (tsun): p'an-tsún(-du) gró-ba (phan tsun(-du) 'gro ba), to walk past, frq.; p'an-tsún-du p'úl-ba (phan tsun du phul ba), to push hither and thither, Glr.; p'an-tsún mt'ún-pai gtam (phan tsun mthun pai gtam), assurances of mutual friendship, Glr.; p'an-tsun gčig-gis gčig-la yi-ge gtóṅ-ba (phan tsun gcig gis gcig la yi ge gtong ba), p'yag byéd-pa (phyag byed pa), gnód-pa byéd-pa (gnod pa byed pa), mutual correspondence, m. greetings, m. encroachment; p'an-tsún sdúr-ba sdébs-pa (phan tsun sdur ba sdebs pa), to compare with one another, to mix one with the other, Zam.; gnyis-gnyis-dag p'an-tsun-gyi _dra-bar yi-ge (gnyis gnyis dag phan tsun gyi 'dra bar yi ge), two equal letters (ă, ā etc.) at a time Gram.: ma-p'áṅ-gi gram p'an-tsún-du (ma phang gi gram phan tsun du) on each of the two shores of lake Ma-paṅ (Ma pang), Mil.; don p'an-tsún bsdu-rgyu yód-pa (don phan tsun bsdu rgyu yod pa), correlative terms, having reciprocal relation, Gram.: p'an-tsún t'or-ba (phan tsun thor ba), to scatter, to disperse; p'an-tsún-dag (phan tsun dag), Cs., both parties. - III. v. the following articles

W. kettle, pot (of tinned copper, the common cooking-vessel in Tibet and India, having the shape of a broad urn); in C. *zaṅs(-bu)* (*zangs(-bu)*), Pers. and Hd. دیگچی (dēgčī); p'an-čuṅ (phan chung), a small vessel of that kind.

I. vb. to be useful: de ni bdag-la mi p'an (de ni bdag la mi phan), that is no more of use to me; p'án-par mi _gyur (phan par mi 'gyur), it will be of no use; bu _di ná-la p'án-par dka (bu 'di na la phan par dka), this son will hardly be useful to me, Glr.; p'án-par dgá-ba-rnams (phan par dga' ba rnams), such as wish to make themselves useful, they who are ready to serve, Thgy.; bgród-la p'an (bgrod la phan), useful for learning to walk, Lt.; nad kún-la p'án-pa yin (nad kun la phan pa yin), that is good for all diseases, Lt.; ṅai nád-la p'án-pa yin-pas (ngai nad la phan pa yin pas), because I have recovered, Glr.; *p'an soṅ* (*phan song*), it has helped, it has got better; ... na p'an (na phan), if ..., then I shall get well, Glr.; p'án-pa žig srid (phan pa zhig srid), recovery might be possible, Pth.; mi p'an (mi phan), it is useless, = hurtful; also: it is not enough, Mil.; mi p'án-par _dód-pa t'ams-čád (mi phan par 'dod pa thams cad), all the malevolent, Domaṅ: k'á-la p'an (kha la phan), lit. 'it is a mere en-
